description: Frontmatter pins model: claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022, a legacy model ID not aliased by the current 'sonnet' shorthand; will break when Anthropic retires it.
suggested_fix: Replace 'claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022' with 'sonnet' to track the current Sonnet model alias.

description: Rule 0 declares 'archivist' as part of the worker tier, but the Available Agent IDs table (lines 35-40) omits archivist entirely; planners cannot legally delegate to it.
suggested_fix: Add '- archivist — Archives session learnings and updates MEMORY.md.' to the Available Agent IDs section.

description: Script is designed for and documents execution via 'curl -fsSL URL | bash'. The script itself is fetched and executed without integrity verification; only the downloaded binary artifact is SHA-256 checked after execution has already begun.
suggested_fix: Provide a checksum-verified download of the installer script itself, or publish signed releases so users can verify the script before executing. Private disclosure required.

description: eval "$(jq -r '...export \(.key)=\"\(.value)\"' scripts/ci-secrets.json)" — JSON key names are not shell-escaped. A tampered ci-secrets.json with a key containing shell metacharacters (semicolons, backticks, $()) would execute arbitrary code.
suggested_fix: Use 'export \(.key)=\(.value | @sh)' and validate key names match ^[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*$ before eval. Private disclosure required for HIGH severity.

description: eval "$(jq -r '...export \(.key)=\(.value | @sh)' $SECRETS_JSON)" — values are @sh-escaped and keys are filtered to VITE_ prefix, but the eval of jq output and the user-controlled $SECRETS_JSON path still introduce risk in multi-user CI environments.
suggested_fix: Confirm SECRETS_JSON points to the expected path before calling jq; consider replacing eval with explicit variable assignments for the small set of known VITE_ variables.

description: eval "$joined" where values are printf %q-escaped but key names from the .env file are not validated. A .env key containing shell special characters would be injected directly into the eval string.
suggested_fix: Validate each key against ^[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*$ before appending to exports; skip or warn on invalid keys.

description: The $FILTER argument (line 11, caller-supplied jq expression) is passed directly into jq without sanitization. A caller providing a malicious jq filter could exfiltrate data or cause unexpected eval behavior.
suggested_fix: Document clearly that $FILTER is trusted-caller-only input; add a comment warning against passing externally-sourced values as the filter argument.

description: postinstall runs install.js which fetches a platform-specific binary from GitHub Releases over HTTPS and places it in bin/. SHA-256 is verified but the download and installation happen automatically on npm install.
suggested_fix: Add an OPENHUMAN_SKIP_INSTALL=1 environment variable guard in install.js so CI and audit environments can npm install without triggering the binary fetch.

description: postinstall: 'husky' runs git hooks setup automatically on npm install. Husky is a well-known tool with small attack surface, but any postinstall constitutes an automatic execution surface.
suggested_fix: No action required unless restricting all auto-execution at install time. Consider 'prepare' script instead of 'postinstall' for husky to avoid running on --production installs.

description: export PATH="$HOME/.cargo/bin:$INSTALL_ROOT/bin:$PATH" prepends using $INSTALL_ROOT which defaults to a caller-controlled path. In shared CI environments, a poisoned INSTALL_ROOT could shadow system binaries.
suggested_fix: Resolve INSTALL_ROOT to a canonical absolute path and reject values containing '..' or paths outside expected directories before using in PATH.

description: Missing name, description, and model frontmatter. File is an internal application prompt loaded via ?raw Tauri imports — intentionally not a Claude Code agent — but lacks any structured NL metadata.
suggested_fix: Add YAML frontmatter with name, description, and model fields if this file is intended to be managed as an NL artifact.

description: No example interaction blocks and no explicit output format section. The agent has a review checklist but no illustration of what a correct review looks like.
suggested_fix: Add at least two example reviews (input diff → output critique) and an Output Format section describing expected response structure.

description: Missing name, description, and model frontmatter on an internal application prompt file.
suggested_fix: Add YAML frontmatter with name, description, and model fields.

description: 'sensible bound' used twice (slippage > a sensible bound, fee > a sensible fraction) — vague thresholds that leave agent behavior undefined.
suggested_fix: Replace with concrete defaults: 'slippage > 3%' and 'fee > 5% of transfer value'; allow these to be configurable.

description: Missing name, description, model frontmatter and no example interaction blocks.
suggested_fix: Add YAML frontmatter and at least two examples showing the agent handling a Composio tool call (happy path and auth error path).

description: Missing name, description, model frontmatter; no examples; no output format section.
suggested_fix: Add frontmatter, two examples (successful polyfill + failure to polyfill), and an Output Format section describing the expected response shape.

description: Missing name, description, and model frontmatter. Has good examples and output format section.
suggested_fix: Add YAML frontmatter with name, description, and model fields.

description: Missing name, description, and model frontmatter. Has good output contract and examples.
suggested_fix: Add YAML frontmatter with name, description, and model fields.

description: Missing name, description, model frontmatter; no examples; no output format section.
suggested_fix: Add frontmatter, interaction examples, and an Output Format section.

description: Missing name, description, model frontmatter and no example blocks despite having an Output Contract section.
suggested_fix: Add YAML frontmatter and examples showing the agent creating a skill and running a validation command.

description: Missing name, description, model frontmatter; no examples; partial output format (tone described but no template).
suggested_fix: Add frontmatter, a sample briefing output, and a structured output template showing sections and max-word targets.

description: Missing name, description, model frontmatter; no examples; no output format; vague 'tight' and 'relevant' quantifiers.
suggested_fix: Add frontmatter, examples, output format, and replace vague terms with concrete constraints.

description: Missing name, description, model frontmatter; no examples; no output format; vague 'genuinely useful' and 'significant' quantifiers.
suggested_fix: Add frontmatter, examples of lessons extracted and their format, and replace vague terms with concrete criteria.

description: Missing name, description, model frontmatter and no examples. Has an Output section.
suggested_fix: Add frontmatter and at least two examples: one react path (memory store) and one escalate path.

description: Missing name, description, model frontmatter. Has good examples and output guidance. Vague: 'as few words as possible', 'relevant context', 'sensible' in scheduling rule.
suggested_fix: Add frontmatter; replace 'as few words as possible' with a token target; define 'relevant context' criteria; specify a concrete default cron format for the scheduling example.

description: Missing name, description, model frontmatter; no examples; no output format section.
suggested_fix: Add frontmatter, two research task examples showing input question and output summary, and an Output Format section.

description: Missing name, description, model frontmatter. Has a detailed Output Format template but no input→output examples showing the format applied to real tool output.
suggested_fix: Add frontmatter and one concrete example showing raw tool output → summary output.

description: Missing name, description, model frontmatter; no examples despite having an Output Format JSON schema. Vague 'complex user goal' and 'simple goals'.
suggested_fix: Add frontmatter, a worked planning example (goal → JSON DAG), and concrete criteria for what makes a goal 'complex' vs 'simple'.

description: Missing name, description, model frontmatter. Has extensive examples and clear behavioral rules.
suggested_fix: Add YAML frontmatter with name, description, and model fields.

description: Excessive vague quantifiers: 'comprehensive' (×2), 'proper' (×2), 'relevant' (results), 'best practices' — abstract standards that cannot be mechanically verified.
suggested_fix: Replace 'comprehensive checks' with an enumerated checklist; replace 'proper type annotations' with 'all exported functions and components have typed parameters and return values'.

description: Vague quantifiers: 'important' (×2), 'relevant', 'significant' — no concrete threshold for what is important enough to persist.
suggested_fix: Define importance criteria: e.g. 'persist only items that would save >10 min of future debugging or change a future architectural decision'.

description: No interaction examples. The agent has a detailed workflow but no illustration of a complete run from input PR number to final report.
suggested_fix: Add one short example showing the agent's final report for a simple PR with one trivial fix applied.

description: No examples and no explicit output format for the agent's final response when the PR is green and clean.
suggested_fix: Add a 'Final output' section describing the one-line summary the agent emits when the exit condition is met, plus one short example.

description: No interaction examples for this agent variant.
suggested_fix: Add one short example showing input PR reference and the resulting final report.

description: Agent delivers build artifacts but the expected response format (what the agent writes back to the orchestrator after a build) is not described.
suggested_fix: Add an 'Output' section describing the agent's reply: build status, artifact paths, and any warnings encountered.

description: Reference-documentation style agent with no stated output format for what it returns after running tests.
suggested_fix: Add an 'Output' section: pass/fail status, failing test names, and relevant log excerpts.

description: No explicit output format for what the agent returns after completing a development task.
suggested_fix: Add an 'Output' section summarizing what was created or modified.

description: No explicit output format for what the agent returns after a deploy action.
suggested_fix: Add an 'Output' section: deployed artifacts, version, platforms, and any post-release checklist items remaining.

description: No explicit output format for what the agent returns after a mobile build or debug task.
suggested_fix: Add an 'Output' section describing the agent's reply structure.

description: Codex command frontmatter lacks an allowed-tools field. The command uses Bash, git, and gh — these should be declared.
suggested_fix: Add 'allowed-tools: Bash, Read, Edit, Write' to the frontmatter.

description: ship-and-babysit has four parallel definitions across .claude/commands/, .agents/agents/, .codex/commands/, and .codex/skills/. The .claude/commands/ version is most complete (ScheduleWakeup, 12-tick cap, fork-owner resolution); others may drift over time.
suggested_fix: Designate .claude/commands/ship-and-babysit.md as the single source of truth and have other definitions reference or import it, or add a diff-check CI step to detect drift.

description: Orchestrator delegates crypto work via 'delegate_do_crypto' while the specialized agent is named 'crypto_agent'. The naming divergence makes the delegation chain harder to audit and trace.
suggested_fix: Verify that 'delegate_do_crypto' is a registered tool that routes to crypto_agent; add a comment in the orchestrator confirming the mapping.
